subject: Continuous Random Variables
topic: §38 The Distribution of a Function of a Random Variable
question: If the probability density of $X$ is given by $f(x) = \begin{cases} 6x(1-x) & 0 < x < 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ find the probability density of $Y = X^3$.
explanation: We have $F(y) = \Pr(Y \le y) = \Pr(X^3 \le y) = \Pr(X \le y^{1/3}) = \int_0^{y^{1/3}} 6x(1-x)\,dx = 3y^{2/3} - 2y$. Hence, $f(y) = F'(y) = 2(y^{-1/3} - 1)$, for $0 < y < 1$ and 0 otherwise.
